text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. Readable code is much easier for you and others to understand. Always use clear naming conventions for your variables and functions; names like `calculateTotal` are better than `ct`. Keep your functions short and focused on a single task. This makes your code easier to test and reuse. Following these practices improves maintainability, allowing you to fix bugs or add features quickly without breaking other parts of the program. In the end, clean code saves time and reduces frustration for everyone working on the project.
